snippets: We also sold an additional ownership stake in our China XD grid business and generated approximately $100 million of pretax proceeds.
aero derivatives are getting a lot more attention now, strong demand growth, a lot of competition. Maybe help us understand your plans for capacity additions in aero derivatives
economics of the larger gas turbines are just so much more economically efficient...heavy-duty gas turbines are going to carry the day
remain on track to meet the runway of 20 gigawatts annualized production by 3Q '26
increased production capacity for valve modules by approximately 40%
Prolec has already launched, completed and/or have investments to expand capacity...valued at $300 million
we also expect to incorporate our lean culture into how we operate these facilities...we are doubling output and adding jobs
we do not feel like we're capacity constrained. They are not capacity constrained to meet the numbers
slot reservation agreements are at higher price and more attractive margins, that will translate to orders in directionally in the next 12 months
Price was positive in all segments
We expect low double-digit revenue growth driven by volume and pricing
directional number of $2,500 [per KW], I think, is a practical illustration of where the market is today
we continue to see price accelerating in gas
we've grown our backlog $16 billion in '25 with $7 billion increase in our backlog in 3Q alone
our backlog expanded to $135 billion, a year-over-year and sequential increase led by both Power and Electrification
equipment backlog grew to $54 billion, up approximately $12 billion year-over-year
Electrification equipment backlog has grown by over $6 billion...now more than $26 billion
Prolec had an equipment backlog of approximately $4 billion at the end of the second quarter
gas turbine backlog grew this quarter from 29 gigawatts to 33 gigawatts and our slot reservation agreements increased from 25 gigawatts to 29 gigawatts

key_takeaways: GE Vernova announced acquisition of remaining 50% of Prolec GE for ~$5.275B (50% cash/50% debt), immediately accretive to EBITDA; Prolec generates $3B revenue at 25% EBITDA margins with $4B equipment backlog and is expected to grow to $4B+ revenue and $1B+ EBITDA by 2028.

Q3 2025 results were strong: orders of $14.6B (+55% YoY), total backlog of $135B, revenue +10% organic, adjusted EBITDA margins expanded 600bps, and free cash flow of ~$730M; full-year guidance reaffirmed with revenue trending toward high end of $36B-$37B range.

Gas Power momentum accelerating with 12 GW of new contracts signed in Q3, gas turbine backlog at 33 GW plus 29 GW slot reservations (62 GW total), targeting ~70 GW by year-end; Electrification revenue guidance raised to ~25% organic growth with EBITDA margin now 14%-15%.
risk_flags: Onshore Wind revenue guidance cut to down high single digits (from down mid-single digits) due to continued softness in U.S. equipment orders; Wind EBITDA losses expected ~$400M for full year.
Tariff headwind estimated at $300M-$400M for 2025 (trending toward lower end net of mitigations), with uncertainty around future tariff policy.
Prolec integration risk: deal expected to close mid-2026, with synergy realization dependent on smooth talent retention, systems integration and operational alignment.
Gas turbine capacity constrained at 20 GW annualized run rate through 3Q 2026; management explicitly not planning capacity expansion beyond that level near-term despite surging demand.
Q4 2025 revenue may be slightly lower year-over-year due to improved gas turbine delivery linearity and continued onshore wind softness.
Corporate costs increasing year-over-year driven by higher stock-based compensation, incentives and increased AI/robotics investments.
